case_summary: Employee notices early signs of disengagement and reduced enthusiasm.

answers.q1: Things are fine I guess, just not as exciting as they used to be.
answers.q2: I'm not tired exactly, just... flat. Like I don't really care either way.
answers.q3: Workload is normal, nothing crazy.
answers.q4: Team is okay. We get along but don't really talk much beyond work stuff.

case_summary: Employee showing classic burnout symptoms with emotional exhaustion and cynicism.

answers.q1: I've started dreading Mondays. I used to love my job but now it feels like a grind.
answers.q2: Emotionally drained. I snap at people more than I should and I feel guilty about it.
answers.q3: The workload is heavy but manageable. It's more the emotional weight of everything.
answers.q4: My team is fine but I've pulled back from them. I don't have the energy to engage.

case_summary: Severe burnout with physical symptoms, complete disengagement and thoughts of quitting.

answers.q1: honestly dont even know where to start. ive been running on empty for months. thinking about just quitting.
answers.q2: cant sleep. constant headaches. cried in the bathroom last week which has never happened before.
answers.q3: way too much. i have no idea how anyone thinks this is sustainable.
answers.q4: everyone is just as burned out as me. we dont talk about it but you can see it.

case_summary: Employee experiencing mild deadline pressure with manageable stress levels.

answers.q1: Just a bit more pressure than usual with an upcoming deadline.
answers.q2: Slightly on edge but nothing I can't handle.
answers.q3: Busy but okay. It'll calm down after next week.
answers.q4: Team is supportive, we're all in the same boat right now.

case_summary: Chronic stress from competing priorities and unclear expectations.

answers.q1: I keep getting pulled in different directions. My manager wants one thing, the client wants another.
answers.q2: Anxious most of the time. Hard to focus. I keep making small mistakes which makes it worse.
answers.q3: It's not the volume, it's the lack of clarity. I don't know what to prioritize.
answers.q4: People are nice but nobody really steps up to resolve the confusion.

case_summary: Acute stress from organizational crisis with severe physical and psychological impact.

answers.q1: Company is going through layoffs and nobody knows who's next. The atmosphere is toxic.
answers.q2: Panic attacks have started. I've never had those before. Can't concentrate at all.
answers.q3: Ironically lighter workload now because projects are on hold but the uncertainty is unbearable.
answers.q4: Team is falling apart. People are updating their CVs openly. Trust is gone.

case_summary: Employee experiencing mild motivational dip after completing a major project.

answers.q1: Just finished a big project and now everything feels a bit anticlimactic.
answers.q2: Low energy. Hard to get started on new things.
answers.q3: Light workload right now which maybe makes it worse.
answers.q4: Team is good. No issues there.

case_summary: Employee feeling undervalued with declining intrinsic motivation.

answers.q1: I work hard but it goes unnoticed. Promotions go to people who are louder not better.
answers.q2: I've stopped going the extra mile. Why bother? It doesn't make a difference.
answers.q3: Workload is fine. I could do more but I've stopped trying.
answers.q4: Team is okay but competitive in an unhealthy way.

case_summary: Complete motivational collapse with active disengagement and presenteeism.

answers.q1: i show up. i do the minimum. i leave. thats it now.
answers.q2: numb mostly. dont feel good or bad about work anymore. just... nothing.
answers.q3: workload doesnt matter when you dont care about any of it
answers.q4: dont really have a team anymore. remote work killed whatever we had.

case_summary: Employee mildly frustrated with inconsistent feedback from manager.

answers.q1: My manager gives feedback but it changes depending on their mood. Hard to know what they really want.
answers.q2: Mildly frustrated but not losing sleep over it.
answers.q3: Workload is fine.
answers.q4: Team works well together independently, we don't rely on the manager much.

case_summary: Toxic leadership causing psychological distress and reduced performance.

answers.q1: My manager publicly criticizes people in meetings. It's humiliating and demoralizing.
answers.q2: I feel anxious before every team meeting. I've started second guessing everything I do.
answers.q3: The work itself is manageable but the fear of being called out makes everything harder.
answers.q4: We don't support each other because we're all just trying to avoid being the next target.

case_summary: Abusive leadership causing trauma responses and high turnover intent.

answers.q1: My manager is abusive. Screaming, personal insults. HR knows and does nothing.
answers.q2: I have nightmares about work. My therapist says I'm showing signs of workplace trauma.
answers.q3: Doesn't matter. Even easy tasks feel impossible in this environment.
answers.q4: Half the team has quit in 6 months. Those of us left are just waiting for a way out.

case_summary: Minor communication gaps in a newly formed remote team.

answers.q1: New team, still figuring out how to work together remotely.
answers.q2: A bit uncertain but not stressed.
answers.q3: Workload is reasonable.
answers.q4: People seem nice but we haven't really gelled yet.

case_summary: Team conflict affecting collaboration and individual wellbeing.

answers.q1: There's a lot of tension between me and a colleague and it's affecting my ability to focus.
answers.q2: I feel anxious going into meetings and I've started avoiding collaboration altogether.
answers.q3: Workload is fine but the toxic atmosphere makes everything harder.
answers.q4: The team is divided, there are clear sides and my manager isn't addressing it at all.

case_summary: Complete team breakdown with interpersonal hostility and isolation.

answers.q1: Someone on my team has been spreading rumors about me. It's become a hostile environment.
answers.q2: I feel isolated and paranoid. I don't trust anyone at work anymore.
answers.q3: Can't focus so everything takes longer than it should.
answers.q4: It's every person for themselves. No collaboration. Just politics.

case_summary: Employee hesitant to share ideas due to mild fear of judgment.

answers.q1: I have ideas but I hold back in meetings because I'm not sure they'll land well.
answers.q2: Just a bit self-conscious. Nothing major.
answers.q3: Workload is fine.
answers.q4: Team is professional but not particularly warm or encouraging.

case_summary: Employee silenced by past negative reactions to speaking up.

answers.q1: Last time I raised a concern I was dismissed in front of everyone. Haven't spoken up since.
answers.q2: I feel invisible. My contributions don't seem to matter.
answers.q3: Normal workload.
answers.q4: The team follows whoever is loudest. Quieter people like me get overlooked.

case_summary: Complete suppression of voice due to fear of retaliation in a high-stakes environment.

answers.q1: I witnessed something unethical but I'm terrified to report it. People who do get pushed out.
answers.q2: Constant dread. I feel complicit but also trapped.
answers.q3: Work piles up because I'm too distracted by the situation to focus.
answers.q4: Culture of silence. Everyone knows but nobody talks.

case_summary: Customer-facing employee experiencing emotional labour fatigue.

answers.q1: I deal with difficult clients all day and have to stay calm and professional no matter what.
answers.q2: By the end of the day I have nothing left emotionally. I'm irritable with my family.
answers.q3: The volume of interactions is high and draining even when nothing goes wrong.
answers.q4: My team gets it but we're all in the same situation so we can't really support each other.

case_summary: Healthcare worker with severe emotional exhaustion and compassion fatigue.

answers.q1: I'm a nurse. I've stopped feeling things when patients are in pain. That scares me.
answers.q2: Detached. Empty. I used to care deeply about my patients. Now I just go through the motions.
answers.q3: Short staffed constantly. The workload is dangerous.
answers.q4: We're all just surviving. Nobody talks about how we actually feel.
